xBRIEFInfo.description: Scope xBRIEF ingested from GitHub issue #76

plan.title: Add ToDo list section with optional reminders

narratives.Description: Add ToDo list section with optional reminders
narratives.Overview:
## Summary

Add a dedicated **ToDo list** section to the Relaxanator UI so users can capture free-text tasks with an optional reminder time, snooze overdue reminders, and see a progressively more prominent reminder banner after the reminder time has passed. Users must be able to edit or delete any item at any time.

## Motivation

Relaxanator already surfaces timed break prompts via a banner stack with snooze/dismiss (`BreakBanner` / break panel). A lightweight personal ToDo list with optional reminders would reuse that mental model for ad-hoc tasks (e.g. “Call to schedule doctor appointment — remind me at 10:00 AM”) without leaving the app.

## Proposed behavior

### ToDo section
- New UI section devoted to the ToDo list (alongside existing panels such as breaks / meditation / noise).
- Users can add items as free text.
- Each item may optionally include a reminder time (local clock time; example: remind at 10:00 AM).
- Users can modify (edit text and/or reminder time) or delete any item at any time.

### Reminders & snooze
- When an item has a reminder time and that time arrives (or is already past), surface a reminder UI.
- Provide a **snooze** action (pattern to consider: existing break-banner snooze UX).
- If the reminder time has passed and the item is still outstanding, increase reminder **prominence progressively** over time (e.g. stronger visual weight / urgency styling as overdue duration grows — exact steps TBD in implementation).

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Dedicated ToDo list section is visible in the main app UI.
- [ ] User can add a free-text ToDo item.
- [ ] User can optionally set a reminder time on an item (e.g. doctor appointment @ 10:00 AM).
- [ ] User can edit item text and/or reminder time after creation.
- [ ] User can delete an item at any time.
- [ ] When reminder time is reached (or past), a reminder affordance appears for that item.
- [ ] User can snooze a reminder; after snooze, the reminder reappears at the snoozed time.
- [ ] After reminder time has passed without completion/dismissal, the reminder becomes progressively more prominent.
- [ ] Basic a11y: reminder affordances use appropriate live-region / role patterns consistent with existing banners where applicable.

## Open questions (non-blocking; do not invent heavy scope)

- **Persistence**: Is `localStorage` (or existing client storage patterns) enough for v1 offline PWA use?
- **Audio cue**: Should overdue ToDo reminders optionally play a cue, or stay visual-only like a quieter sibling of break prompts?
- **Timezone / clock**: Confirm local device time only for v1 (no calendar TZ complexity).
- **Snooze duration**: Fixed interval (e.g. match break snooze minutes) vs user-configurable?
- **Completion vs dismiss**: Distinct “done” vs “dismiss reminder but keep item” actions?

## Out of scope (unless later scoped)

- Sync across devices / accounts
- Recurring ToDos / full calendar integration
- Push notifications beyond what the app already does for breaks (unless explicitly added later)

## Related existing UX

- `src/components/BreakBanner.tsx` — banner + snooze / dismiss / acknowledge actions
- Break panel + notification helpers — timed prompt surface patterns to align with visually and behaviorally

items.title: Dedicated ToDo list section is visible in the main app UI.
User can add a free-text ToDo item.
User can optionally set a reminder time on an item (e.g. doctor appointment @ 10:00 AM).
User can edit item text and/or reminder time after creation.
User can delete an item at any time.
When reminder time is reached (or past), a reminder affordance appears for that item.
User can snooze a reminder; after snooze, the reminder reappears at the snoozed time.
After reminder time has passed without completion/dismissal, the reminder becomes progressively more prominent.
Basic a11y: reminder affordances use appropriate live-region / role patterns consistent with existing banners where applicable.
